principle: No v1.0.0 tag or release may exist until one Windows machine has cloned, hydrated, verified, built, played, exported, resumed, snapshotted, and signed the local operator receipt from the exact estate pair.

automated.failure: A required local tool, supported version, free port, or writable estate path is unavailable.
A checkout is dirty, diverged, missing the required ancestor, or not on the locked branch.
npm lockfile installation or local Chromium hydration fails.
The estate lock is invalid, the Arc/World vendored plane differs, or either checkout is not clean.
Two clean builds of either repository produce different file-tree digests.
TypeScript, complete tests, deterministic reference generation, or production build fails.
TypeScript, complete tests, production build, Gate 6, or Gate 7 fails.
Any desktop or mobile browser journey fails on the local machine.
Git bundles, source archives, builds, cartridges, publications, receipts, or checksums cannot be assembled and verified.

human.prompt: Did AXM Arc open locally and show a playable authored cartridge?
Did Rodoh open locally and show the five first-party cartridge entries?
Did you enter at least one bundled cartridge, make a visible player decision, resolve a contract, and see the recorded result?
Did The Orchard at Low Tide import as holder-owned, remain outside the Program-of-Record registry, and display its unfamiliar vocabulary?
Did you export an exact run, clear the holder context, import that run, and resume the same state?
Did the reviewed four-book Codex and Addenda bundle open locally and verify against its recorded SHA-256?
Did the local estate snapshot contain both Git bundles, both source archives, both static builds, the cartridge library, the publication bundle, receipts, and checksums?
